design_philosophy.core_loop: Match colored Puyos in groups of 4+ to clear them; create chains to send garbage blocks to opponent; survive until opponent's grid fills
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time decision-making under pressure; predicting opponent's chain responses; managing board space efficiently
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a skilled spell-caster (Arle Nadja) defeating progressively stronger opponents through puzzle mastery and strategic chain-building
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Blend of Tetris-style falling blocks with Dr. Mario-style matching mechanics; introduces competitive offset system where players counter each other's attacks rather than taking passive damage; story-driven single-player campaign against 12 unique opponents before final boss

difficulty.continues_system: Fixed sequence of opponents in story mode; progression through tournament-style bracket
difficulty.checkpointing: Linear opponent progression in single-player story; defeat triggers restart against same opponent or return to previous checkpoint
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Offset mechanic allows skilled players to counter attacks and regain tempo; chain mastery is primary skill lever; multiple game modes (Single, Double, Endless, Mission) offer varied difficulty curves

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode unlocks via sequential opponent defeats; 12 main opponents + Dark Prince final boss; multiple courses available
replayability_hooks: Competitive Double mode against human players with different skill levels
52 Mission mode challenges with puzzle constraints
Multiple difficulty courses through same opponent sequence

world_building_techniques: Character-driven opponent roster with individual motivations (Rulue loves Dark Prince; others 'pull shenanigans' with Arle)
Clear narrative framing: stop Dark Prince from taking over world
Quest structure: defeat 12 opponents then face final boss to save world and return home

boss_design.philosophy: Progressive difficulty via fixed sequence of 12 unique opponent characters; final opponent (Dark Prince) serves as narrative climax and skill check
notable_bosses: Dark Prince (final boss; antagonist seeking world domination)
Rulue (motivated by love for Dark Prince rather than his orders)
11 other named opponents with individual personalities and behaviors

faction_indicators: Dark Prince's faction (antagonist force trying to take over world)
Arle Nadja as lone hero resisting Dark Prince
Rulue's conflicted position (loves Dark Prince but must be defeated)
danger_signals: Dark Prince's explicit goal to 'take over the world'
Tournament structure where player must defeat all opponents in sequence
environmental_storytelling: Opponent selection reflects different character archetypes and motivations
Story mode narrative arc: gather strength through opponents, confront final evil
Characters originate from Madō Monogatari RPG universe, implying broader world context
